meta.title: GCP-ADP-2: BigQuery Pipelines, Governance and Reporting

questions.stem: A team needs to **land raw files cheaply and durably before analysis**. Which service or feature is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because it is Google Cloud's managed object storage service for raw files and staging areas

**B** is incorrect because it is not the primary managed governance layer for organizing cloud data estates

**C** is incorrect because it is not the standard BigQuery object for reusable SQL definitions over existing tables

**D** is incorrect because it is not the standard serverless option for recurring BigQuery SQL execution

[Cloud Storage overview](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/introduction)

questions.stem:
Which service or feature is most directly associated with the following need?

- Goal: land raw files cheaply and durably before analysis
- Constraint: use the managed vendor capability when possible

What should the team choose?
questions.explanation:
**C** is correct because it is Google Cloud's managed object storage service for raw files and staging areas

**A** is incorrect because it is not the standard serverless option for recurring BigQuery SQL execution

**B** is incorrect because it is not the primary managed governance layer for organizing cloud data estates

**D** is incorrect because it is not the standard BigQuery object for reusable SQL definitions over existing tables

[Cloud Storage overview](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/introduction)

**C** is correct because teams should choose it when they need to land raw files cheaply and durably before analysis

**A** is incorrect because it is not the primary managed governance layer for organizing cloud data estates

questions.stem: A team needs to **query files in Cloud Storage without loading them into native BigQuery storage**. Which service or feature is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**C** is correct because they let BigQuery query supported external data such as Cloud Storage files

**A** is incorrect because it is not the managed Google Cloud service for Apache Beam data pipelines

**B** is incorrect because it is not the GoogleSQL construct for flattening repeated arrays into rows

**D** is incorrect because it is not the primary managed governance layer for organizing cloud data estates

[BigQuery external tables](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/external-tables)

questions.stem: A team needs to **stream rows into BigQuery in near real time**. Which service or feature is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because it is the managed API for high-throughput programmatic streaming ingestion into BigQuery

**B** is incorrect because it is not the primary managed governance layer for organizing cloud data estates

**C** is incorrect because it is not the managed Google Cloud service for Apache Beam data pipelines

**D** is incorrect because it is not the GoogleSQL construct for flattening repeated arrays into rows

questions.stem: A team needs to **flatten repeated array elements into rows for analysis**. Which service or feature is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**D** is correct because `UNNEST` expands repeated values so nested arrays can be queried relationally

**A** is incorrect because it is not the Looker Studio feature for creating derived metrics in reports

**B** is incorrect because it is not the primary managed governance layer for organizing cloud data estates

**C** is incorrect because it is not the managed Google Cloud service for Apache Beam data pipelines

[Work with arrays in GoogleSQL](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/arrays)

questions.stem: A team needs to **save reusable query logic without storing another physical copy of the data**. Which service or feature is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**D** is correct because views store SQL definitions rather than duplicating base-table data

**A** is incorrect because it is not the BigQuery feature for querying supported files in Cloud Storage directly

**B** is incorrect because it is not the primary BigQuery streaming-ingestion API in this scenario

**C** is incorrect because it is not the main Google Cloud BI/reporting tool in this use case

[BigQuery views](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/views-intro)

**C** is incorrect because it is not the standard serverless option for recurring BigQuery SQL execution
